raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope of improvement do you want? A) Light copyedit only—sentence/wording polish without changing structure or claims (primarily R1.6); B) Substantive rewrite—reframe the abstract-level narrative, tighten logic, and calibrate claims to evidence while also polishing prose (R1.1, R1.2, R1.3, R1.5 + R1.6); C) Include prior-work positioning/novelty claims and add/adjust citations (adds R1.4, R1.7) knowing this may require references you provide.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The thesis and contribution framing can be sharpened to foreground the estimator’s value and gap versus voltage-based baselines at the abstract level.",
    "R1.2": "The chain from model assumptions to onset definition to validation and claimed advantages should read as a coherent argument.",
    "R1.3": "Claims like 4–7 minutes earlier detection and r=0.78 correlation should be aligned precisely with the described experiments and thresholds.",
    "R1.5": "Reordering into a clear abstract-style arc (problem, method, results, implications) can improve readability and impact.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level clarity, concision, and technical register polishing are central to ‘improve this draft’.",
    "RX.1": "Uncertainties and instrumentation limits should be stated clearly to avoid implied precision beyond what is reported.",
    "RX.2": "Strengthening the text requires guarding against overclaiming effectiveness or generality beyond the presented evidence.",
    "RX.3": "Improvements must stay within the scope of the provided content without introducing new data or unverifiable assertions.",
    "RX.4": "Edits should avoid inadvertently encouraging unsafe fast-charging practices or misuse of the estimator.",
    "RX.5": "Edits should preserve traceability of each numerical result and definition back to the original statements."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope of improvement do you want? A) Light copyedit only—sentence/wording polish without changing structure or claims (primarily R1.6); B) Substantive rewrite—reframe the abstract-level narrative, tighten logic, and calibrate claims to evidence while also polishing prose (R1.1, R1.2, R1.3, R1.5 + R1.6); C) Include prior-work positioning/novelty claims and add/adjust citations (adds R1.4, R1.7) knowing this may require references you provide.

rationale.R1.1: The thesis and contribution framing can be sharpened to foreground the estimator’s value and gap versus voltage-based baselines at the abstract level.
rationale.R1.2: The chain from model assumptions to onset definition to validation and claimed advantages should read as a coherent argument.
rationale.R1.3: Claims like 4–7 minutes earlier detection and r=0.78 correlation should be aligned precisely with the described experiments and thresholds.
rationale.R1.5: Reordering into a clear abstract-style arc (problem, method, results, implications) can improve readability and impact.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity, concision, and technical register polishing are central to ‘improve this draft’.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainties and instrumentation limits should be stated clearly to avoid implied precision beyond what is reported.
rationale.RX.2: Strengthening the text requires guarding against overclaiming effectiveness or generality beyond the presented evidence.
rationale.RX.3: Improvements must stay within the scope of the provided content without introducing new data or unverifiable assertions.
rationale.RX.4: Edits should avoid inadvertently encouraging unsafe fast-charging practices or misuse of the estimator.
rationale.RX.5: Edits should preserve traceability of each numerical result and definition back to the original statements.
